learning_objectives.Construct a well-structured static webpa: Demonstrate the ability to use header, nav, main, article, section, aside, footer tags appropriately.
learning_objectives.Implement interactive forms with various: Create a contact form including text, email, number, checkbox, radio, and submit inputs with required attributes.
learning_objectives.Embed and control multimedia content (im: Integrate an image gallery, an audio player, and a video player using HTML5 tags.
learning_objectives.Apply basic accessibility principles to: Ensure all images have descriptive alt text and form inputs have associated labels.

learning_objectives.Style HTML elements using various CSS se: Apply styles to elements based on their tag, class, ID, and attribute.
learning_objectives.Manipulate the box model properties to c: Adjust margins, padding, and borders to create desired layouts and visual separation.
learning_objectives.Customize text appearance with fonts, si: Implement custom web fonts and control text alignment, decoration, and transformation.
learning_objectives.Create basic page layouts using Flexbox: Arrange navigation items horizontally and content sections vertically using Flexbox properties.

learning_objectives.Implement media queries to adapt layouts: Create distinct CSS rules for mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports.
learning_objectives.Design and build a website using a mobil: Start styling for smaller screens and progressively enhance for larger screens.
learning_objectives.Ensure images and other media scale appr: Utilize CSS properties like max-width: 100% and srcset for responsive images.
learning_objectives.Utilize relative units for scalable and: Apply 'em', 'rem', 'vw', and 'vh' units for typography and element sizing.

learning_objectives.Write JavaScript code to declare variabl: Create variables for strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and objects, and perform arithmetic and logical operations.
learning_objectives.Implement conditional logic and loops to: Use if/else statements and switch cases for decision making, and for/while loops for iteration.
learning_objectives.Define and invoke functions to encapsula: Create functions with parameters and return values to perform specific tasks.
learning_objectives.Interact with the Document Object Model: Select elements, change their text content, modify attributes, and attach event listeners for user interactions.

learning_objectives.Initialize a Git repository and track ch: Use 'git init', 'git add', and 'git commit' commands to manage project history.
learning_objectives.Create and manage branches for feature d: Demonstrate branching workflows for new features and bug fixes.
learning_objectives.Connect a local Git repository to a remo: Push local commits to GitHub and pull changes from a remote repository.
learning_objectives.Collaborate on a project using GitHub by: Submit a pull request, review code, and successfully merge changes.

stages.name: Advanced JavaScript & ES6+
topics: Arrays and Array Methods (map, filter, reduce)
ES6+ Features (Arrow Functions, Template Literals, Spread/Rest Operators)

learning_objectives.Utilize advanced array methods to transf: Apply map, filter, and reduce to process arrays of objects or numbers.
learning_objectives.Implement modern JavaScript features (ES: Use arrow functions, template literals, and destructuring in practical scenarios.
learning_objectives.Handle asynchronous operations using Pro: Fetch data from an API and display it on a webpage using modern asynchronous patterns.
learning_objectives.Implement robust error handling mechanis: Use try/catch blocks to gracefully manage potential errors in asynchronous operations.

learning_objectives.Inspect and modify HTML and CSS in real-: Change element styles and attributes directly in the browser to test design variations.
learning_objectives.Utilize the Console panel for logging in: Debug JavaScript code by printing variable values and testing functions.
learning_objectives.Debug JavaScript code effectively using: Identify and fix logical errors in JavaScript functions by tracing execution flow.
learning_objectives.Analyze network requests and responses u: Monitor API calls, check request/response headers, and identify performance bottlenecks.

learning_objectives.Create reusable functional components us: Develop a component hierarchy for a simple application like a to-do list or counter.
learning_objectives.Pass data between components using props: Design components that accept and display dynamic data passed from parent components.
learning_objectives.Manage component-specific state using th: Implement interactive features where component data changes based on user actions.
learning_objectives.Handle user interactions (clicks, input: Attach event listeners to JSX elements and update component state accordingly.

learning_objectives.Manage side effects and data fetching us: Fetch data from an external API when a component mounts and update state.
learning_objectives.Implement client-side navigation using R: Create a multi-page React application with distinct routes and navigation links.
learning_objectives.Manage form inputs and submissions using: Build a user registration or login form with input validation.
learning_objectives.Develop custom hooks to encapsulate reus: Create a custom hook for common functionalities like form handling or local storage interaction.

stages.name: Frontend Tooling & Optimization
topics: Basic Performance Optimization (Code Splitting, Image Optimization)

learning_objectives.Manage project dependencies and scripts: Install, update, and remove packages, and define custom scripts in package.json.
learning_objectives.Set up a modern development environment: Configure a basic Vite project for a React application.
learning_objectives.Configure ESLint to enforce code style a: Integrate ESLint into a project and resolve linting warnings.
learning_objectives.Apply basic performance optimization tec: Implement lazy loading for components and optimize image assets.

stages.name: State Management with Redux Toolkit
topics: Connecting React Components to Redux Store

learning_objectives.Understand the core principles of Redux: Explain the Redux data flow and its components (actions, reducers, store).
learning_objectives.Implement global state management in a R: Configure a Redux store and create slices for different parts of the application state.
learning_objectives.Handle asynchronous operations (e.g., AP: Fetch data from an API and update the Redux store with the fetched data.
learning_objectives.Connect React components to the Redux st: Display data from the Redux store and update it via user interactions.
